data.title: MGP Ingredients appoints CFO to the additional post of interim president and CEO
data.description:
• MGP Ingredients (NASDAQ:MGPI) announced on Friday the appointment of CFO Brandon Gall to the additional post of interim president and CEO, effective January 1, 2025.
  • MGPI shares were trading -0.07% pre-market at $41.77.
  • Gall succeeds David Bratcher, who is set to resign from the board of the spirits and food ingredient solutions provider on December 31.
  • Bratcher has agreed to remain available for advisory services to facilitate a smooth transition.
  • Additionally, Donn Lux, a board member, will succeed Karen Seaberg as chairman.
  • Lux will lead the board search for a permanent CEO.
  • Seaberg will remain a board member.
  • Also, MGP Ingredients has reaffirmed its 2024 sales and adjusted earnings guidance.
  • The 2024 capital expenditures are expected to be ~$72M, down from the prior expectations of $78M.
  • The Q4 and full-year results are expected to be announced in late February 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Mastercard, Broadcom, Southwest Airlines, Waste Management, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Waste Management (NYSE:WM) and Mastercard (NYSE:MA) as well as declarations from companies such as Xcel Energy (XEL) and State Street (STT).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) and Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Waste Management (WM)

  • Dividend declared: December 16
  • New dividend amount: $0.825
  • Previous amount: $0.75
  • Increase: 10%

Mastercard (MA)

  • Dividend declared: December 17
  • New dividend amount: $0.76
  • Previous amount: $0.66
  • Increase: 15.2%

Lamb Weston (LW)

  • Dividend declared: December 19
  • New dividend amount: $0.27
  • Previous amount: $0.26
  • Increase: 2.8%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Xcel Energy (XEL)

  • Dividend declared: December 18
  • Dividend amount: $0.5475

Winnebago (WGO)

  • Dividend declared: December 18
  • Dividend amount: $0.34

New York Times (NYT)

  • Dividend declared: December 19
  • Dividend amount: $0.13

State Street (STT)

  • Dividend declared: December 19
  • Dividend amount: $0.76

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Broadcom (AVGO)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 23
  • Payout date: December 31

Philip Morris (PM)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 26
  • Payout date: January 13

Southwest Airlines (LUV)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 26
  • Payout date: January 16

American Tower (AMT)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 27
  • Payout date: February 3

data.title: Oracle dips after Monness cuts rating to Sell
data.description:
Shares of Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) fell about 2% premarket on Friday after Monness downgraded the stock to Sell from Neutral with a $130 price target.

The analysts said that tapping into the generative AI mania of 2024, Oracle's stock has outperformed Monness' universe this year, and is on track for the best performance since 1999. Thus, Oracle's current price-to-earnings, or P/E, multiple is about double its long-term, historical average.

The analysts added that despite Wall Street's confidence heading into Oracle's second quarter fiscal 2025 call last week, the company delivered surprisingly uninspiring results. They added that Monness' current fiscal 2025 EPS estimate for Oracle now stands exactly where it was one year ago, while its Cloud Services revenue projection is lower.

In addition, Monness finds Oracle's plan to double capital expenditure in fiscal 2025 to be troubling and unsustainable.

The analysts said the public cloud landscape is daunting, a field dominated by three, large, innovative, deep-pocketed Big Tech companies with a powerful, ever-expanding generative AI arsenal, combined with a deep cloud portfolio.

Amazon's (AMZN), Amazon Web Services, Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google Cloud Platform and Microsoft's (MSFT) Azure are considered to be the top three biggest cloud service providers in the U.S.

data.title: Accenture slips even as Mizuho ups price target after 'strong' Q1
data.description:
• Accenture (NYSE:ACN) was in focus on Friday as Mizuho Securities upped its price target on the IT services company after it reported "strong" first-quarter results.
  • Shares fell 0.5% in premarket trading.
  • "Our two main takeaways are that ACN is gaining market share (we point to our proprietary opex analysis) and that we expect a continued growth recovery as we view DOGE worries as likely overblown due to the company's mission-critical nature of its federal services," analyst Dan Dolev wrote in a note to clients, referring to the Department of Government Efficiency initiative enacted by President-elect Trump.
  • Dolev upped his price target to $428 from $395 on Accenture and reiterated his Outperform rating.
  • Looking at the quarter, Dolev said there was growth across every end market and an upward revision to guidance, which suggests that organic growth is rising. Other positives include "strong" year-over-year growth in financial services, products, and consulting, as well as "sustained momentum" in generative artificial intelligence.

data.title: AAR to divest Landing Gear Overhaul business to GA Telesis
data.description:
• Aviation services provider AAR (NYSE:AIR) is set to divest its Landing Gear Overhaul business to GA Telesis for a transaction value of $51M.
  • AIR shares were trading +1.63% Friday pre-market at $61.59.
  • The company has entered into a definitive agreement for the divestiture.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025.
  • The company said the divestiture will be immediately accretive to margins and earnings.
  • AAR said the divestiture is a part of its strategic plan to optimize its portfolio by investing in core functions that will accelerate its targeted growth and margin expansion initiatives.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Family ties: A new generation of Waltons has voting rights at Walmart
data.description:
The wealthiest family in the U.S. is shaking things up in terms of who has voting rights with Walmart (NYSE:WMT). While the leadership and board roles held by members of the Walton family are unchanged, the younger generation of the family is being set up to have more control in the future.

The former voting members were the children of the legendary Sam Walton (Rob Walton age 80, Jim Walton age 76, and Alice Walton age 75). The voting block has now been expanded to a total of 11 family members, all of whom are grandchildren of Sam Walton. The generation of voting family members includes Benjamin Walton, Carrie Walton Penner, Samuel Walton, James Walton, Steuart Walton, Thomas Walton, Alice Proietti, and Lukas Walton.

"As part of their commitment to Walmart’s success, the Walton family believes responsible ownership requires thoughtful planning for the future," read a statement from Walton Enterprises. "Moving forward, internal governance of their ownership interest will expand to include members of the next generation of their family."

The Walton family holds a 46% stake in Walmart (WMT) that is worth more than $340 billion.

Sam Walton opened Walton's 5&10 in Bentonville, Arkansas in 1950 and launched the first Walmart store in Rogers, Arkansas in 1962. Walton died in 1992 after seeing Walmart (WMT) become the largest retailer in the world.

data.title: Travel & Leisure CFO Michael Hug to retire in 2025
data.description:
• Travel & Leisure Co (NYSE:TNL) chief financial officer Michael Hug announced his intention to retire during the fiscal 2025 year, according to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • Hug will step down in his role as CFO effective either as of the earlier of a date that is mutually agreeable to Hug and the company after a successor has been found, and June 1, 2025. He will continue to serve as CFO until his retirement date.
  • Hug came to Travel & Leisure from Wyndham (WH) where he served as CFO of Wyndham’s timeshare business. Wyndham acquired Travel & Leisure in 2021.

data.title: Stock index futures tumble as Trump-backed bill fails to pass, fueling shutdown risks
data.description:
Stock index futures tumbled on Friday, after a stopgap spending plan backed by President-elect Donald Trump was rejected in the House of Representatives, further fueling concerns of a government shutdown.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.7% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.55%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.29%.

"The big question now is whether a U.S. government shutdown is about to happen," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The latest bill was voted down by 235 votes to 174. The funding is set to expire on Friday night.

"A short-lived shutdown affects government workers, but has limited economic impact. The longer a shutdown lasts, the more disruptive it is to the U.S. economy. Economic data may not get published in a shutdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Wall Street's bounce back on Thursday following its worst session in over three months ran out of steam in the final hour of trading.

"For those wanting a quiet run up to Xmas, the good news is that there hasn't been any real follow-through to the Fed-induced slump on Wednesday. The bad news is that an initial recovery in markets struggled to gain traction yesterday," Reid added.

Traders will also keep an eye on the November PCE price index data. Core PCE is expected to come in at 0.2% M/M and 2.9% Y/Y.

PCE is forecasted to come in at 2.5% Y/Y and 0.2% M/M.

"U.S. personal income and consumption data should continue to signal rising real incomes. The fact that household incomes are so strong, allowing consumption without use of savings or credit card debt, gives a solid foundation for US economic growth in 2025," Donovan added.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Sanofi to buy China rights to Cytokinetics lead asset
data.description:
• Cytokinetics (NASDAQ:CYTK) announced Friday that Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) would buy exclusive rights to its lead candidate, aficamten, in Greater China from its partner, Corxel Pharmaceuticals.
  • The company didn’t disclose the financial terms of the agreement between but said it remains on track to receive up to $150M in milestone payments and low-to-high teens on aficamten sales from the French drugmaker.
  • Cytokinetics (NASDAQ:CYTK) added that it is entitled to receive additional undisclosed payments linked to the agreement between the two.
  • In 2020, Shanghai-based Corxel teamed up with Cytokinetics (CYTK) to develop aficamten in Greater China.
  • The oral cardiac myosin inhibitor is currently under priority review at China’s National Medical Products Administration as a treatment for obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, a disease of heart muscles.

data.title: VICI Properties closes $750M senior unsecured notes offering
data.description:
• VICI Properties (NYSE:VICI) has closed its public offering of $750M in aggregate principal amount of 5.125% senior unsecured notes due 2031.
  • The notes were issued at 99.643% of par value and will mature on November 15, 2031.
  • Source: Press Release
